major: Latin American and Latino Studies Minor

courses.title: Introduction to Latin American and Latino Studies

notes: Students majoring in a different program of study may find that a minor in LALS is an invaluable addition to their educational plan. Prospective transfer students interested in minoring in LALS are encouraged to complete two lower-division LALS courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring; see the “Preparation for the Minor” section below. Please visit the Latin American and Latino Studies (LALS) website to learn more about this minor. Prospective transfer students who would like to connect with an LALS advisor are strongly encouraged to email lalsadvising@ucsc.edu .

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
